description_long: This object is a hand-forged knife with a dark, weathered appearance, likely made from carbon steel. The blade is single-edged, broad, and tapers to a sharp point, showing signs of hammer marks and a rough, matte finish. The handle is a continuous extension of the blade, twisted into a spiral or scroll shape, providing both grip and a decorative flourish. There is no visible guard separating the blade from the handle, giving it a seamless, rustic look. The entire knife is constructed from a single piece of metal, emphasizing durability and traditional blacksmithing techniques. The coloration is predominantly dark gray to black, with subtle variations due to forging and oxidation.
description: A hand-forged, single-piece steel knife with a broad, pointed blade and a twisted, scroll-shaped handle, finished in dark, weathered tones.
description_view_0: The knife is seen from the side, clearly showing the broad blade and the twisted, scroll-like handle.
description_view_1: A narrow, edge-on view reveals the thinness of the blade and the twisted profile of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view displays the blade's curvature and the full spiral of the handle.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, emphasizing the linearity of the blade and the compactness of the twisted handle.
attribute_handle_design: twisted, scroll-shaped, integral steel, unwrapped, decorative spiral
